article_meta.title: Federal immigration officers board an elevator with respondents at immigration court in New York on 17 November 2025

reasoning: The response avoids quoting-attribution violations but largely misidentifies neutral or contextual author prose as bias, mislabels types, and misses clear, stronger author-level bias (e.g., the loaded verb 'spied' in the headline/lede). Overall, several false positives and notable false negatives.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged items are from the author's prose; no direct quotes or reproduced statements were improperly flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Several flagged items are neutral contextual statements mislabeled as omission or slant (e.g., court access note; claims of repeated accusations). The negativity bias item is debatable but not clearly biased.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clear author-level loaded wording like 'The FBI spied on a private Signal group chat' and potentially 'arrests have escalated,' as well as framing like 'enabling federal agents to then arrest.'
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels frequently mismatched: using Bias by Omission and Slant where evidence isn’t shown; better fits might have been Omission of Source Attribution or none. Only the negativity item is arguably defensible.
